competency_groups.title: Relations and Functions
competency_groups.description: Understanding types of relations, equivalence relations, one-to-one and onto functions.
competencies: Identify reflexive, symmetric, transitive, and equivalence relations
Determine if a function is one-to-one or onto

competency_groups.title: Inverse Trigonometric Functions
competency_groups.description: Defining inverse trigonometric functions, understanding their domains, ranges, principal value branches, and graphing.
competencies: Define inverse trigonometric functions with domain and range
Identify principal value branches of inverse trigonometric functions
Graph inverse trigonometric functions

competency_groups.title: Matrices
competency_groups.description: Understanding matrix concepts, operations (addition, multiplication, scalar), properties including non-commutativity, transpose, symmetric/skew-symmetric matrices, and invertibility.
competencies: Classify types of matrices: zero, identity, square, rectangular, symmetric, skew-symmetric
Perform matrix addition, multiplication by a scalar, and matrix multiplication
Demonstrate non-commutativity of matrix multiplication with examples
Find the transpose of a matrix
Determine invertibility of matrices and prove uniqueness of inverse

competency_groups.title: Determinants
competency_groups.description: Calculating determinants up to 3x3, understanding minors and co-factors, finding area of a triangle, adjoint/inverse matrices, and solving systems of linear equations.
competencies: Calculate determinant of square matrices (up to order 3)
Compute minors and co-factors
Find the area of a triangle using determinants
Determine adjoint and inverse of a matrix
Analyze consistency, inconsistency, and number of solutions for systems of linear equations
Solve systems of linear equations in two or three variables using matrix inversion

competency_groups.title: Continuity and Differentiability
competency_groups.description: Understanding continuity, differentiability, chain rule, derivatives of composite/implicit/inverse trigonometric functions, exponential/logarithmic differentiation, parametric forms, and second-order derivatives.
competencies: Test for continuity and differentiability of a function at a point
Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions
Differentiate inverse trigonometric functions (sin^-1 x, cos^-1 x, tan^-1 x)
Derive derivatives of implicit functions
Differentiate exponential and logarithmic functions including logarithmic differentiation
Find derivatives of parametric forms

competency_groups.title: Applications of Derivatives
competency_groups.description: Applying derivatives to rate of change, increasing/decreasing functions, and finding maxima/minima using first and second derivative tests.
competencies: Calculate the rate of change of quantities
Determine intervals where a function is increasing or decreasing
Find local maxima and minima using the first derivative test (geometric motivation)
Apply the second derivative test to identify extrema

competency_groups.title: Integrals
competency_groups.description: Understanding integration as the inverse of differentiation, techniques (substitution, partial fractions, parts), evaluating specific integral forms, Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, and properties of definite integrals.
competencies: Recognize integration as the inverse process of differentiation
Apply substitution method for integration
Use partial fraction decomposition to integrate rational functions
Integrate by parts
Evaluate standard integral forms involving square roots and quadratic denominators
State and apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (without proof)
Utilize basic properties of definite integrals for evaluation

competency_groups.title: Application of Integrals
competency_groups.description: Calculating the area under simple curves including lines, circles, parabolas, and ellipses in standard form.
competencies: Calculate the area bounded by a line or circle using integration
Determine areas enclosed by parabolas and ellipses (standard forms only)

competency_groups.title: Differential Equations
competency_groups.description: Defining differential equations, understanding order and degree, finding general/particular solutions, solving by separation of variables, homogeneous equations, and linear forms.
competencies: Identify the order and degree of a differential equation
Differentiate between general and particular solutions
Solve first-order differential equations using method of separation of variables
Solve homogeneous differential equations of first order and first degree
Solve linear differential equations of type dy/dx + py = q (p, q functions of x or constants)
Solve linear differential equations of type dx/dy + px = q (p, q functions of y or constants)

competency_groups.title: Vectors
competency_groups.description: Understanding vectors and scalars, magnitude/direction, types of vectors (equal, unit, zero, parallel, collinear), position vectors, components, vector operations (addition, scalar multiplication), dot product, and cross product.
competencies: Distinguish between vectors and scalars
Calculate magnitude and direction cosines/ratios of a vector
Classify types of vectors: equal, unit, zero, parallel, collinear
Find position vectors and components of vectors
Perform addition of vectors and multiplication by a scalar
Apply properties of dot product (scalar) and cross product (vector)

competency_groups.title: Three-Dimensional Geometry
competency_groups.description: Finding direction cosines/ratios of lines, equations of lines (Cartesian/Vector), skew lines, shortest distance between lines, and angles between lines.
competencies: Determine direction cosines and ratios for a line joining two points
Formulate Cartesian and vector equations of a line in 3D space
Identify skew lines

competency_groups.title: Linear Programming Problem
competency_groups.description: Understanding terminology (constraints, objective function), graphical method for 2-variable problems, feasible/infeasible regions, and optimal solutions.
competencies: Define constraints, objective functions, optimization, feasible, infeasible, and optimal solutions

competency_groups.title: Probability
competency_groups.description: Understanding conditional probability, multiplication theorem, independent events, total probability, and Bayes' theorem.
competencies: Calculate conditional probabilities
